records.ety: [OE. repairen, OF. repairier to return, fr. L. repatriare to return to one's contry, to go home again; pref. re- re- + patria native country, fr. pater father. See Father, and cf. Repatriate.]

quotes: I thought . . . that he repaire should again.

defs: To go; to betake one's self; to resort; ass, to repair to sanctuary for safety.
quotes: Go, mount the winds, and to the shades repair.

records.ety: [OF. repaire retreat, asylum, abode. See Repair to go.]

defs: The act of repairing or resorting to a place.
quotes: The king sent a proclamation for their repair to their houses.

defs: Place to which one repairs; a haunt; a resort.
quotes: There the fierce winds his tender force assail And beat him downward to his first repair.

records.ety: [F. rparer, L. reparare; pref. re- re- + parare to prepare. See Pare, and cf. Reparation.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Repaired (-p?rd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Repairing.]

defs: To restore to a sound or good state after decay, injury, dilapidation, or partial destruction; to renew; to restore; to mend; as, to repair a house, a road, a shoe, or a ship; to repair a shattered fortune.
quotes: Secret refreshings that repair his strength.
Do thou, as thou art wont, repair My heart with gladness.

defs: To make amends for, as for an injury, by an equivalent; to indemnify for; as, to repair a loss or damage.
quotes: I 'll repair the misery thou dost bear.

defs: Restoration to a sound or good state after decay, waste, injury, or partial restruction; supply of loss; reparation; as, materials are collected for the repair of a church or of a city.
quotes: Sunk down and sought repair Of sleep, which instantly fell on me.

defs: Condition with respect to soundness, perfectness, etc.; as, a house in good, or bad, repair; the book is out of repair.
